name: Proud Ground
notes: Oregon's largest CLT; 75%+ BIPOC homeowners; buyers purchase at 20–50% below market

name: Sabin CDC
notes: Northeast Portland affordable housing and community development

name: RootedHomes
notes: Sustainable and permanently affordable housing in Central Oregon

name: DevNW
notes: Affordable homeownership in Cottage Grove and Florence, OR

name: Homestead Community Land Trust
notes: Affordable housing with climate equity and energy efficiency focus

name: Kulshan Community Land Trust
notes: Affordable homeownership and housing counseling in Whatcom County

name: OPAL Community Land Trust
notes: Island affordability in the San Juans

name: Vashon HouseHold
notes: Island affordability on Vashon

name: Tahoma Community Land Trust
notes: BIPOC and low-income housing focus

name: Spokane Community Land Trust
notes: Permanent affordable housing in Eastern Washington

name: San Juan Community Home Trust
notes: Affordable homeownership on San Juan Island

name: Africatown Community Land Trust
scale: Liberty Bank Building (100+ units), Africatown Plaza (130 units)
notes: Black Diaspora community preservation in Seattle's Central District; ground-floor retail for Black-owned businesses

name: Lopez Community Land Trust
notes: Model hybrid CLT; net-zero housing; 20+ year sustainable agriculture internship program; founded after housing costs rose 190% in one year

name: Bay Community Land Trust
notes: Sustainable housing, intentional communities, regenerative farming

name: Evergreen Land Trust
notes: Cooperative communities, sustainable land use; oldest CLT in Washington

name: Waldron Community Land Trust
notes: Sustainable agriculture and affordability on remote island

name: South of the Sound Community Land Trust
notes: Local food systems and farmland preservation in Thurston County

name: Puget Sound Agrarian Commons
notes: Food sovereignty and farmland viability; part of Agrarian Trust network

name: Champlain Housing Trust
notes: Largest CLT in the U.S.; merged with Lake Champlain Housing Dev Corp in 2006; received $20M MacKenzie Scott gift 2023; 160,000+ sq ft nonresidential space

name: Houston Community Land Trust
notes: Post-Hurricane Harvey formation; $52.7M city investment 2021; partnership with Houston Land Bank

name: Sawmill Community Land Trust
notes: Formed to reclaim contaminated industrial site (former particleboard factory); founding acquisition = catalyst model

name: City of Lakes Community Land Trust
notes: Launched Commercial Land Trust Initiative ~2017; incubated Partnership in Property CLT

name: East Harlem/El Barrio Community Land Trust
notes: First NYC CLT to receive city land and capital financing; $13M deal for 4 city-owned buildings

name: Rondo Community Land Trust
scale: 34 affordable senior apartments + 9,300 sq ft commercial space
notes: Minnesota's first commercial CLT; acquired Golden Thyme Café; Selby Ave corridor; city land at nominal cost

name: Urban Land Conservancy
notes: Combines CLT with land bank functions; acquires in gentrifying areas; Dearfield Fund for Black Wealth

name: Crescent City Community Land Trust
notes: Commercial corridors + housing; Pythian building co-development (69 apartments + commercial); BIPOC entrepreneur focus

name: Partnership in Property CLT
notes: Spun out of City of Lakes CLT pilot (2017); perpetually affordable commercial ownership for BIPOC small business owners

name: Dudley Neighbors Inc.
scale: 60 acres; 225 homes; urban farm; greenhouse; charter school
notes: Only U.S. community org with eminent domain authority; multi-ethnic tripartite governance model

name: Berkshire Community Land Trust
notes: Founded by Robert Swann and Susan Witt; designed from day one to include retail sites and Main Street character properties

name: New Communities Inc.
notes: First CLT in the United States; founded by SNCC organizers; at one time largest Black-owned landholding in America; $12.8M USDA discrimination settlement 2009

name: Agrarian Trust
scale: 12 founding Agrarian Commons; 2,400 acres across 10 states
notes: Long-term equitable leases for regenerative agriculture; next-gen farmer and BIPOC land access focus

name: Athens Land Trust
scale: 1.2-acre West Broad Community Garden; West Broad Farmers' Market
notes: Operated farmers market on leased school district land; 13,000+ lbs produce/year; 200+ weekly shoppers; market on hiatus 2025 — critical lesson for MCLT

name: Montavilla Community Land Trust
scale: 0.84 acres (proposed acquisition at 7700 SE Stark St)
notes: PROPOSED. Organizing to acquire Montavilla Farmers Market lot listed at $4,999,000. Year-round market pavilion + mass timber community hub + commissary kitchen + cold storage.
